vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-compliant with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the pots appear to contain only substrate and debris. P3 (Pothos) is absent; the white rabbit anchor is present but the plant is missing.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present, but no Pothos foliage visible.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or lacking organic matter. No signs of active growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) in the foreground pot. A white cylindrical object (possibly a small bottle or container) is present near the yellow pot.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, non-biological environment. There is no evidence of plant life in any of the frames. The only changes are minor shifts in lighting and the position of the white cylindrical object.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: All specimens are confirmed as 'Dead/Lost'. The containers are currently serving as inert vessels for substrate only.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline registry and compare it against the visual data. Upon validation, it is clear that the biome is currently devoid of the expected botanical specimens. The 'plants' are not merely struggling; they are absent. The environment is static, and the presence of non-botanical debris suggests the desk is being used for storage rather than cultivation. The 'white rabbit' anchor is the only consistent element, yet it sits in a pot devoid of its assigned Pothos.
